project.name: Peria
project.tagline: Human-readable by default. LLM-ready by design.
project.description: Peria compiles code, configuration, docs, and Git history into a living technical wiki with traceable claims.
project.audience: Engineers maintaining a codebase and AI agents that need source-backed context before making changes.
project.problem: Most generated docs flatten a repo into indexes. Peria should preserve intent, ownership boundaries, and change history.
project.currentFocus: Make the repository capable of documenting itself with a useful markdown wiki, Fumadocs-compatible output, graph artifact, and agent context map.
highlights: The human wiki in /docs is the source of truth.
The Fumadocs-compatible content is generated from markdown instead of a parallel content model.
Agent context files point back to the same wiki tree used by humans.
Git metadata is part of the generated knowledge, not an afterthought.

peria.audience: Contributors running repo-wide build, typecheck, test, and release preparation commands.
responsibilities: Expose root scripts that validate every package.
Carry the self-documentation config for this repository.

@peria/core.audience: Contributors changing extraction, graph, config, or wiki generation behavior.
responsibilities: Extract packages, modules, exports, commands, adapters, features, context files, and Git metadata.
Generate markdown pages, manifest data, llms.txt content, and graph artifacts.

@peria/cli.audience: Developers running Peria locally or wiring it into scripts.

@peria/adapters.audience: API teams embedding generated docs in framework applications.
responsibilities: Expose framework-specific entrypoints for Express, Fastify, and NestJS.
Keep adapter contracts thin until generated artifacts are stable.

@peria/sdk.audience: Tools that need to call Peria without going through the CLI.
responsibilities: Prepare for embedded server and graph/search access.

pages.title: Peria Overview
pages.description: Peria compiles code, configuration, docs, and Git history into a living technical wiki with traceable claims.

pages.description: Workspace packages, manifests, scripts, dependencies, and package exports.

pages.description: Fumadocs-compatible documentation generated into /docs and backed by markdown pages.

pages.description: A compact reading map for agents, derived from the human wiki.

pages.description: Mermaid diagrams generated by the same engine used by the peria diagram command.

pages.title: Development Map
pages.description: Maintenance-oriented map of packages, modules, entrypoints, and likely change areas.

pages.description: Generated release-readiness summary based on package metadata and wiki output.

pages.title: Known Gaps
pages.description: Detected gaps that keep the generated wiki from being a complete adoption artifact.
